title: Word Break
description: Given a string s and a dictionary of strings wordDict, determine if s can be segmented into a space-separated sequence of one or more dictionary words. For example, the string 'leetcode' can be segmented as 'leet' 'code'.

examples.explanation: The string 'leetcode' can be segmented into 'leet' and 'code', which are both in the dictionary.
The string 'applepenapple' can be segmented into 'apple', 'pen', and 'apple', all of which are present in the dictionary.
The string 'catsandog' cannot be segmented completely using the provided dictionary as there is no valid segmentation for 'og'.

constraints: The length of s is at most 300.
Each word in wordDict consists of lowercase English letters.
hints: Try to use dynamic programming to keep track of the possible segmentations.
Consider using a boolean array to represent whether a substring can be formed from the dictionary.
Think about how you can build up solutions for larger substrings using results from smaller substrings.
You might want to check each prefix of the string against the dictionary to see if it can lead to a valid segmentation.
